tesseract.js — vendored (OCR driver)
====================================
Upstream:   https://github.com/naptha/tesseract.js
Package:    https://www.npmjs.com/package/tesseract.js
Version:    6.0.1  (driver)
Runtime:    tesseract.js-core 6.1.2 (core WASM) + @tesseract.js-data/eng 1.0.0
            (English model) — NOT vendored; opt-in SRI-pinned downloads, see
            peerd-runtime/pdf/ocr-store.js OCR_ASSETS.
License:    Apache-2.0

Status: VENDORED
----------------
The two driver files are committed and SRI-pinned (below), and the runtime
core+lang SRIs are pinned in OCR_ASSETS (ocr-store.js) — so hasValidOcrSris()
is true and OCR is shippable: Settings → Voice & OCR offers the opt-in
download, and a scanned PDF (read_pdf engine:'ocr'/'auto') escalates to
recognition. The whole path stays fail-closed: a missing driver, an SRI
mismatch, or a recognizer error falls back to the pdf.js text layer + the
honest "looks scanned" note (offscreen/pdf-extract.js).

REMAINING: a real-browser smoke-test (see "Validate locally") — the only step
that can't be done headless. Recommended before relying on OCR in a release.

Why vendored
------------
tesseract.js is the OCR engine for scanned/image-only PDFs (offscreen/
pdf-extract.js → extractViaOcr). Like pdf.js, the DRIVER is vendored code (it
runs as the browser loaded it — no build step). The two HEAVY assets it loads
at runtime — the core WASM and the English language model — are NOT vendored;
they're the opt-in, SHA-384 SRI-pinned runtime download in
peerd-runtime/pdf/ocr-store.js (the Moonshine-voice pattern). Vendoring the
multi-MB core+lang would bloat the package for the majority of users who never
OCR a PDF.

Files committed (the npm `dist/` minified ESM build)
----------------------------------------------------
  tesseract.esm.min.js  — the main API (createWorker, …). Lazy-imported by
                          offscreen/pdf-extract.js (loadTesseract); paid once,
                          on the first scanned PDF.
  worker.min.js         — the worker script createWorker spawns; loaded via the
                          workerPath option as an extension URL ('self' under
                          the offscreen CSP).

Do NOT commit the core WASM or any *.traineddata — those are the runtime
download (ocr-store.js). The .map files and the CommonJS build are omitted.

Subresource Integrity (SHA-384)
-------------------------------
  tesseract.esm.min.js  sha384-C+b3kNXqc/kbk8HRFKxpQIN52IuwyPg1zTyLFtZb3cvnSIRHL6+4p+cwjkQA/jLS
  worker.min.js         sha384-Xz8ctHtZgH/eWwOqd6igOU/kepnilz4dowW84REE2KL9DtDbxEnd/xxYhzBhNCzd

CSP (done)
----------
The offscreen page runs the tesseract worker (vendored, 'self') which loads the
core WASM + language model from blob: URLs (ocr-store's IDB-cached bytes, via
pdf-extract.js blobUrlFor). The extension_pages CSP now carries
`worker-src 'self' blob:` and `blob:` in connect-src across all channels
(manifests/base.json + dev.patch.json + preview.patch.json) so those loads pass.

Validate locally (the one step that needs a browser)
----------------------------------------------------
  - Smoke-test read_pdf with engine:'ocr' on a scanned PDF, then 'auto' on the
    same file (it should escalate), then 'auto' on a born-digital PDF (it should
    NOT escalate). Confirm the worker's blob:/wasm loads pass the CSP.
  - If the language loader insists on appending `<lang>.traineddata.gz` to a
    base path rather than taking the blob: URL directly, adjust the langPath
    wiring in pdf-extract.js (extractViaOcr).

Update procedure
----------------
  1. Pick the target driver version V from https://registry.npmjs.org/tesseract.js
  2. curl -sSL "https://registry.npmjs.org/tesseract.js/-/tesseract.js-V.tgz" -o tess.tgz
  3. tar -xzf tess.tgz
  4. cp package/dist/tesseract.esm.min.js package/dist/worker.min.js \
       extension/vendor/tesseract/
  5. Recompute SHA-384 and paste above:
       openssl dgst -sha384 -binary FILE | openssl base64 -A
  6. Update the Version line. If the core/lang versions change, update their
     EXACT-pinned URLs in OCR_ASSETS + compute-ocr-sri.sh and re-run
     scripts/compute-ocr-sri.sh to repin those SRIs.
  (scripts/vendor-tesseract.sh automates steps 2-5.)
